pages.title: What Is Account Currency? — Definition and Control Discipline
pages.meta_description: Account currency assigns ERP custody to balances—State 04—for reconciliation, FX close, and audit reconstruction—not superficial ISO styling.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — primary SEO anchor for account currency

pages.title: Transaction Currency vs Account Currency vs Reporting Currency
pages.meta_description: The same financial event can carry three distinct currency identities. Understanding the difference between transaction, account, and reporting currency is critical to multi-currency accounting accuracy.
pages.seo_intent: comparison intent — high-value professional SEO

pages.title: Account Currency in ERP Systems — Reference Architecture
pages.meta_description: Account currency configuration inside ERP systems governs how balances are maintained, revalued, reconciled, and reported. A reference architecture for enterprise finance practitioners.
pages.seo_intent: ERP application intent — practitioners searching for system-specific currency behavior

pages.title: Functional Currency Explained — Definition, Determination, and Control
pages.meta_description: Functional currency is the currency of the primary economic environment in which an entity operates. Its relationship to account currency, ledger currency, and reporting currency is critical to multi-currency accounting.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — functional currency, IFRS IAS 21, accounting standards

pages.title: Foreign Currency Revaluation and Translation — AccountCcy.com
pages.meta_description: Foreign currency revaluation and translation are the mechanisms through which account currency balances are converted into ledger, reporting, and consolidation currencies. Understanding the difference is critical.

pages.meta_description: CFO governance layer for monetary truth—ERP custody, FX revaluation vs translation, reporting integrity, audit reconstruction—not treasury FX alone.

pages.meta_description: Currency translation converts measured results into presentation currency—State 06 visibility discipline.

pages.meta_description: Consolidation adjustment records group-level reshaping—eliminations, translation ties, ownership mechanics—after subsidiary proofs exist.

pages.meta_description: Currency exposure names monetary risk spanning quotation, obligation, settlement, and reporting—where custody ambiguity amplifies FX outcomes.

pages.title: Exchange Rate Source — AccountCcy.com Glossary
pages.meta_description: Exchange rate source names the governed authority for FX rates—revaluation, translation, consolidation—not informal screenshots or desk anecdotes.

pages.title: Period-End Revaluation — AccountCcy.com Glossary
pages.meta_description: Period-end revaluation applies governed FX rates at reporting cut-off to open monetary balances—binding account custody to ledger measurement outcomes.

pages.title: Realized FX — AccountCcy.com Glossary
pages.meta_description: Realized FX names exchange differences crystallized through settlement, payment, receipt, or closure—not unrealized revaluation on still-open balances.

pages.title: Remeasurement — AccountCcy.com Glossary
pages.meta_description: Remeasurement restates monetary items when measurement currency or accounting basis changes—not presentation translation dressed as operational FX hygiene.

pages.title: Translation Adjustment — AccountCcy.com Glossary
pages.meta_description: Translation adjustment accumulates equity impacts when financial statements convert into reporting currency—distinct from ledger revaluation mechanics.

pages.title: Unrealized FX — AccountCcy.com Glossary
pages.meta_description: Unrealized FX recognizes exchange movement before settlement—typically via period-end revaluation governed by rate lineage on monetary items.

pages.title: Audit Defensibility — AccountCcy.com Glossary
